key_drivers: Iran war driving WTI crude to $90.77 with geopolitical supply disruption (+3.8M barrel EIA build is a bearish counterpoint but structural disruption dominates); stagflation risk elevated
FOMC decision today (March 18) — near-certain hold at 3.50-3.75% (98.1% per CME FedWatch); dot plot expected to signal fewer 2026 cuts amid oil shock; VIX falling from 22.37 to 22.03 pre-open
RBA back-to-back hike to 4.1% (March 17) offset by China official PMI contraction (49.0 in February) and risk-off environment; AUD scores BEARISH (-4) as commodity-currency headwinds dominate

data_quality.notes: Report generated at 0758 EST on March 18, 2026 — pre-FOMC decision session. VIX direction scored as FALLING (22.37 → 22.03 pre-open, -1.52%). MOVE direction scored as FALLING (-7.06% on March 17). EIA official inventory data is from week ending March 6 (crude +3.8M barrel build); API data for week ending March 14 showed +6.56M build (per prior report). Gold ETF flows: Commerzbank and JPMorgan both noted ETF outflows since Iran war began; gold price ~$5,004-5,010/oz. EUR/USD at ~1.1480 (down from 2026 high of 1.2080). AUD/USD at ~0.7060 (rebounding from RBA hike). USD/JPY at ~158-159. BoJ holding steady; ECB holding at 2.0%. Pre-FOMC Day 2 uncertainty reduces confidence by 1 point on rate-sensitive instruments (ES, NQ, YM, RTY, M6E, 6J).
